intent: Manual, source-backed synergy metadata seed consumed by the app as a curated scoring layer on top of the heuristic model.

keengamer.label: KeenGamer Ironclad Guide
strengths: Useful package coverage for new StS2 cards

packages.description: Named archetypes used by the scorer.

cards.description: Per-card curated metadata using project card keys.

strength.description: Stack Strength and convert it into burst damage, especially with multi-hit attacks.
heuristics: Reward multi-hit attacks when deck already has repeatable Strength scaling.
Reward Strength generators more when deck already has multi-hit finishers.
Add extra value when Vulnerable support is present.

block.description: Accumulate Block and convert it into damage or repeated payoff triggers.
heuristics: Body Slam is weak without dense or persistent Block generation.
Barricade and Entrench become much better once the deck already has meaningful Block density.
Passive Block sources increase package value disproportionately.

exhaust.description: Use Exhaust as a resource engine for draw, Block, and finishers.
heuristics: Corruption, Dark Embrace, and Feel No Pain form the main engine and should score as premium only with enough exhaust fuel.
Exhaust payoffs should be penalized in low-exhaust decks.
Support cards like Burning Pact and True Grit should gain value if the deck already contains exhaust payoffs.

self_damage.description: Spend HP as a resource, then turn that HP loss into Strength, AoE, draw, or tempo.
heuristics: Do not rate HP-loss cards highly together by default; the deck needs mitigation, sustain, or explicit payoffs.
Rupture becomes much stronger once repeated HP-loss enablers exist.
Bloodletting specifically wants draw, energy sinks, or payoff density.

vulnerable.description: Apply Vulnerable consistently, then exploit it for burst, scaling, defense, or draw.
heuristics: Vulnerable payoffs should require actual Vulnerable application density.
Vulnerable application deserves a bonus in Strength and burst decks even without full package commitment.

strike.description: Exploit cards that count or replay Strike-tagged attacks, without overcommitting to low-impact cards.
heuristics: Perfected Strike scales with Strike count, but not every Strike card is worth adding.
Strike decks need enough draw, energy, or secondary scaling to avoid falling off.

StS2_Ironclad-Bash.title: Bash
notes: Baseline Vulnerable source.
Gets extra value when the deck already has Vulnerable payoffs or Strength burst.

StS2_Ironclad-Thunderclap.title: Thunderclap
notes: Efficient way to spread Vulnerable across multiple enemies.
Useful support card for AoE and burst decks, not just dedicated Vulnerable builds.

StS2_Ironclad-Uppercut.title: Uppercut
notes: Good utility add when the deck needs both Weak and Vulnerable.
Needs enough energy support to offset its heavier cost.

StS2_Ironclad-Vicious.title: Vicious
notes: One of the clearest payoff cards in the Vulnerable package.
Should score poorly when the deck has weak Vulnerable application.

StS2_Ironclad-Dominate.title: Dominate
notes: Bridge card between Vulnerable and Strength scaling.
Needs reliable debuff setup before it becomes premium.

StS2_Ironclad-Cruelty.title: Cruelty
notes: High ceiling in decks already built around applying Vulnerable and hitting hard.
Should be treated as a synergy pick, not a generic damage card.

StS2_Ironclad-BodySlam.title: Body Slam
notes: Core payoff for the Block package.
Also benefits indirectly from exhaust decks that generate large Block through Feel No Pain.

StS2_Ironclad-Barricade.title: Barricade
notes: Engine card that needs surrounding Block density to justify its setup cost.
Should score much higher once Body Slam, Entrench, or persistent Block sources exist.

StS2_Ironclad-Entrench.title: Entrench
notes: Classic win-more card unless the deck already creates substantial Block.
Should be strongly penalized in thin-block decks.

StS2_Ironclad-Juggernaut.title: Juggernaut
notes: Damage engine for Block-heavy decks.
Cross-package bridge because exhaust decks often generate Block repeatedly.

StS2_Ironclad-Corruption.title: Corruption
notes: Main exhaust engine card.
Should spike in value alongside Dark Embrace, Feel No Pain, and a skill-heavy shell.

StS2_Ironclad-DarkEmbrace.title: Dark Embrace
notes: One of the main exhaust payoffs and a clear support-gated pick.
Should be rated as weak standalone value in low-exhaust decks.

StS2_Ironclad-FeelNoPain.title: Feel No Pain
notes: Premium exhaust payoff and bridge into Block strategies.
Should sharply improve Body Slam and Juggernaut evaluations once supported.

StS2_Ironclad-BurningPact.title: Burning Pact
notes: Good support card on its own, but much better with exhaust payoffs.
Should gain additional weight if the deck already contains Dark Embrace or Feel No Pain.

StS2_Ironclad-SecondWind.title: Second Wind
notes: Powerful cross-package support card.
Gets much better in skill-heavy or exhaust-engine decks and helps enable Block payoffs.

StS2_Ironclad-AshenStrike.title: Ashen Strike
notes: Late-turn or late-fight exhaust finisher.
Should be kept below generic premium attacks until exhaust density is present.

StS2_Ironclad-PactsEnd.title: Pact's End
notes: Very support-gated payoff.
Should get a strong activation threshold in the scorer instead of a flat bonus.

StS2_Ironclad-Rupture.title: Rupture
notes: Defining payoff for self-damage decks.
Should be valued very differently depending on whether repeated HP-loss enablers already exist.

StS2_Ironclad-Inferno.title: Inferno
notes: Acts as both repeatable HP-loss enabler and repeatable payoff.
Needs enough survivability or healing to avoid becoming a trap.

StS2_Ironclad-Hemokinesis.title: Hemokinesis
notes: Good standalone frontload, but should improve further once self-damage payoffs exist.
Needs a deck-level HP-cost cap to avoid overcommitting to risky cards.

StS2_Ironclad-Bloodletting.title: Bloodletting
notes: Support-gated card; strongest guidance from sources is that it wants draw or a clear payoff shell.
Local card description file appears incomplete for this card, so metadata should remain mechanic-oriented rather than numeric.

StS2_Ironclad-Offering.title: Offering
notes: Broadly strong card that also acts as package glue for self-damage and exhaust shells.
Should not require full self-damage commitment to score well.

StS2_Ironclad-Inflame.title: Inflame
notes: Reliable generic Strength source.
Gets a larger bonus in decks that already contain multi-hit attacks or Strength multipliers.

StS2_Ironclad-DemonForm.title: Demon Form
notes: Premier long-fight Strength source.
Should be less attractive in already-clunky decks without time or defense to set up.

StS2_Ironclad-FightMe.title: Fight Me!
notes: Compact card that both generates and exploits Strength.
Should be treated as stronger when the deck already wants Strength scaling.

StS2_Ironclad-Whirlwind.title: Whirlwind
notes: One of the clearest Strength payoffs due to repeated hits.
Should scale heavily with Strength sources and energy support.

StS2_Ironclad-TwinStrike.title: Twin Strike
notes: Good early card with upside in both Strength and Strike shells.
Should not be overvalued in Strike decks if overall defense and scaling are weak.

StS2_Ironclad-PerfectedStrike.title: Perfected Strike
notes: Core payoff card for Strike decks.
Sources agree this should not justify taking every Strike-tagged card blindly.

StS2_Ironclad-Hellraiser.title: Hellraiser
notes: High-upside Strike payoff that wants draw support.
Should stay niche unless the deck is clearly leaning into Strike density and hand cycling.

StS2_Ironclad-Aggression.title: Aggression
notes: Recurring attack support for longer fights and attack-heavy decks.

StS2_Ironclad-Anger.title: Anger
notes: Fast early damage card that mainly benefits low-curve or attack-spam decks.

StS2_Ironclad-Armaments.title: Armaments
notes: Generic stabilizer that improves strong upgraded payoffs without needing a dedicated package.

StS2_Ironclad-BattleTrance.title: Battle Trance
notes: Pure consistency card that helps setup-heavy or expensive hands play more smoothly.

StS2_Ironclad-BloodWall.title: Blood Wall
notes: Efficient mitigation that also turns on self-damage payoffs.

StS2_Ironclad-Bludgeon.title: Bludgeon
notes: Raw frontload finisher that wants enough draw or energy support to carry its cost.

StS2_Ironclad-Brand.title: Brand
notes: Glue card that cross-connects self-damage, exhaust, and Strength shells.

StS2_Ironclad-Break.title: Break
notes: High-impact Vulnerable source that also supplies immediate damage.

StS2_Ironclad-Breakthrough.title: Breakthrough
notes: Strong early AoE that incidentally advances self-damage plans.

StS2_Ironclad-Bully.title: Bully
notes: Simple Vulnerable payoff that should stay mediocre without debuff density.

StS2_Ironclad-Cascade.title: Cascade
notes: High-variance top-deck acceleration that improves when the deck is dense with hits.

StS2_Ironclad-Cinder.title: Cinder
notes: Standalone damage card that picks up extra value in exhaust shells.

StS2_Ironclad-Clash.title: Clash
notes: Conditional damage card that falls off hard unless the deck is unusually streamlined.

StS2_Ironclad-Colossus.title: Colossus
notes: Defensive tech card that gets better when the deck already applies Vulnerable reliably.

StS2_Ironclad-Conflagration.title: Conflagration
notes: AoE payoff for turns where the deck chains multiple attacks together.

StS2_Ironclad-CrimsonMantle.title: Crimson Mantle
notes: Excellent bridge card because it pays you off for HP loss while stabilizing the fight.

StS2_Ironclad-Defend.title: Defend (Ironclad)
notes: Baseline block card; useful for heuristics but not a dedicated package signal on its own.

StS2_Ironclad-DemonicShield.title: Demonic Shield
notes: Niche block-conversion card that also interacts with self-damage triggers.

StS2_Ironclad-Dismantle.title: Dismantle
notes: Gets much better once the deck can apply Vulnerable consistently and can also exploit extra hits.

StS2_Ironclad-DrumofBattle.title: Drum of Battle
notes: Slow exhaust engine support piece that improves deck flow over longer combats.

StS2_Ironclad-DualWield.title: Dual Wield
notes: Card-copy tool that is only as good as the attacks or powers already worth duplicating.

StS2_Ironclad-EvilEye.title: Evil Eye
notes: Solid block card that upgrades sharply once the deck can exhaust cards on demand.

StS2_Ironclad-ExpectaFight.title: Expect a Fight
notes: Local text is incomplete, but it appears to scale with attack-dense hands rather than standing alone.

StS2_Ironclad-Feed.title: Feed
notes: Fatal payoff that improves long-run survivability without needing a dedicated synergy shell.

StS2_Ironclad-FiendFire.title: Fiend Fire
notes: Premium exhaust finisher that wants enough hand quality and redraw support to convert its cost.

StS2_Ironclad-FlameBarrier.title: Flame Barrier
notes: Reliable premium block card that also improves block-payoff shells.

StS2_Ironclad-ForgottenRitual.title: Forgotten Ritual
notes: Local text is incomplete, but the trigger clearly rewards regular exhausting.

StS2_Ironclad-Grapple.title: Grapple
notes: Block payoff that should only rise once the deck can generate Block repeatedly within a turn.

StS2_Ironclad-Havoc.title: Havoc
notes: Variance-heavy cheat card that becomes safer in cleaner decks and exhaust shells.

StS2_Ironclad-Headbutt.title: Headbutt
notes: Flexible recursion tool that improves consistent access to the deck's best cards.

StS2_Ironclad-HowlfromBeyond.title: Howl from Beyond
notes: Recurring AoE payoff that is much more attractive when the exhaust engine is already online.

StS2_Ironclad-Impervious.title: Impervious
notes: Massive single-card defense that also feeds block payoffs cleanly.

StS2_Ironclad-InfernalBlade.title: Infernal Blade
notes: Cheap tempo card that plays better in decks that want free attacks or extra exhaust triggers.

StS2_Ironclad-IronWave.title: Iron Wave
notes: Clean early tempo card that lightly contributes to block shells.

StS2_Ironclad-Juggling.title: Juggling
notes: Attack-chain payoff that wants the deck to play multiple cheap attacks each turn.

StS2_Ironclad-Mangle.title: Mangle
notes: Standalone damage plus enemy debuff, mainly valued as frontload and safety.

StS2_Ironclad-MoltenFist.title: Molten Fist
notes: Burst payoff that sharply improves once the deck can stack Vulnerable.

StS2_Ironclad-One-TwoPunch.title: One-Two Punch
notes: Attack-duplication payoff that spikes when the deck already has premium attack hits or Strength.

StS2_Ironclad-Pillage.title: Pillage
notes: Consistency card that becomes stronger the more attack-dense your deck is.

StS2_Ironclad-PommelStrike.title: Pommel Strike
notes: One of the best generic Strike-tagged support cards because it improves both damage flow and card flow.

StS2_Ironclad-PrimalForce.title: Primal Force
notes: Specialized transformation effect that wants a deck with attacks worth converting rather than generic value.

StS2_Ironclad-Pyre.title: Pyre
notes: Local text is incomplete, but it appears to be a slower scaling effect rather than frontload.

StS2_Ironclad-Rage.title: Rage
notes: Attack-density block engine that helps bridge aggressive decks into block payoffs.

StS2_Ironclad-Rampage.title: Rampage
notes: Self-scaling attack that improves with redraw and fight length rather than a named package.

StS2_Ironclad-SetupStrike.title: Setup Strike
notes: Excellent bridge card because it contributes to both Strike density and Strength scaling.

StS2_Ironclad-ShrugItOff.title: Shrug It Off
notes: Premium glue card for almost any slower deck and especially strong in block shells.

StS2_Ironclad-Spite.title: Spite
notes: Clean reward for light self-damage packages because it turns HP loss into both damage and cards.

StS2_Ironclad-Stampede.title: Stampede
notes: Delayed attack-conversion payoff that prefers decks packed with good attack hits.

StS2_Ironclad-Stoke.title: Stoke
notes: Big exhaust engine card that wants a hand full of non-attacks worth converting into draw.

StS2_Ironclad-Stomp.title: Stomp
notes: Attack-chain AoE payoff that gets dramatically easier to deploy in aggressive low-curve decks.

StS2_Ironclad-StoneArmor.title: Stone Armor
notes: Persistent defense source that helps make slower block decks more reliable.

StS2_Ironclad-Strike.title: Strike (Ironclad)
notes: Baseline Strike support that mainly matters as fuel for Strike-count payoffs.

StS2_Ironclad-SwordBoomerang.title: Sword Boomerang
notes: Classic multi-hit Strength payoff that should improve with any reliable Strength source.

StS2_Ironclad-Tank.title: Tank
notes: Highly specialized defensive tradeoff card that should stay heavily penalized without real mitigation.

StS2_Ironclad-Taunt.title: Taunt
notes: Good bridge card because it contributes both defense and Vulnerable support in one slot.

StS2_Ironclad-TearAsunder.title: Tear Asunder
notes: Major payoff once the deck can repeatedly lose HP and leverage the extra hits.

StS2_Ironclad-Thrash.title: Thrash
notes: Attack finisher that gains a real ceiling when the deck has attacks worth exhausting or recurring.

StS2_Ironclad-Tremble.title: Tremble
notes: Low-overhead Vulnerable source mainly valued for enabling stronger follow-up cards.

StS2_Ironclad-TrueGrit.title: True Grit
notes: One of the best exhaust support cards because it also contributes meaningful defense.

StS2_Ironclad-Unmovable.title: Unmovable
notes: Block amplifier that should only become attractive once the deck already generates large card-based Block.

StS2_Ironclad-Unrelenting.title: Unrelenting
notes: Tempo attack that improves decks looking to chain a second premium attack in the same turn.
